scope: Focused positive tid follow-up across 446-455, with second-sample and third-sample confirmation on the newly surfaced 453 branch
verdict: The new 446-455 anonymous follow-up surfaces a previously unrecorded API1 positive branch on tid=453.

Unlike canonical API1 tid=431, tid=453 currently behaves as a cover-shell-only positive branch: errorno=0 and non-empty cover title/type persist across 3 public CIDs, but video_ids stay empty on all confirmed samples.
API1 tid=453 therefore upgrades from an untested band point into a caller-visible noncanonical positive cover-shell branch, but not into a field-equivalent replacement for tid=431.
Across the same 3 public samples, API2 tid=453 stays on the key-all-illegal branch in both XML and JSONP, so the new 453 branch is currently API1-only rather than a cross-interface positive family.

The flanking tids 446-452 and 454-455 remain on the ordinary -111005 branch on both interfaces in the primary anonymous sample, so the current best reading is a narrow local API1-positive island at 453 rather than a wider newly opened band.

api1_453.title: non-empty on all 3 confirmed public CIDs
api1_453.interpretation: API1-only positive cover shell without video_ids

remaining_open_questions: Whether API1 has another positive tid beyond 431 and 453 remains open.
Whether API2 has another positive tid outside 535/540/541 remains open.
Whether API1 tid=453 keeps the same cover-shell-only shape under aged-cookie or login-state replay remains open.
